description:
Advances: mutagen.cli, mutagen.mutation_enumeration

Closes **F28 / CF11** (MEDIUM consensus from Security + Performance — Security M1, Performance F-PERF-07/12).

CLI accepts unlimited repetition of `--scope` / `--tests`. The enumerator materializes every site in memory before the runner starts. No aggregate budget. Per-site timeout doesnt bound the whole run. Three avenues for runaway resource use.

## Deliverable

- Cap `--scope` / `--tests` repetition at 100 each. Reject excess with `:too_many_targets`.
- Cap total enumerated sites at a sensible upper bound (e.g. 10,000). Configurable via `--max-sites`.
- Add `--budget-ms <N>` for aggregate wall-clock budget. When exceeded, the runner terminates gracefully and emits a partial JSON report with `truncated: true`.
- Fix `phase_scope` O(n²): the `acc ++ records` accumulation in lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:311-323 — prepend-then-reverse. (This is a half-overlap with F20 / `.25`; do it here since its at the same touch points.)
- Revise `.spec/specs/cli.spec.md` + `.spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md` accordingly.

## Verification

- New cli_test.exs: 101 scopes → `:too_many_targets`; 10,001 sites → `:too_many_sites`; budget elapsed → partial report
- Benchmark: `phase_scope` with 1000 records — assert O(n) not O(n²) via timing or operation count
- mix test green

## Allowed touches

- lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (phase_scope + aggregate budget)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex (site cap)
- .spec/specs/cli.spec.md, mutation_enumeration.spec.md
- Corresponding test files
- README.md, CHANGELOG.md

## Out of Scope

- Streaming the enumerator (Stream.transform conversion) — that is F44 / `.25` (AST/perf mega)
- NDJSON output streaming — that is F17 / `.30`

## Merge gate
- [ ] mix test green / strict pre-merge green
- [ ] Cap-enforcement tests pass
- [ ] phase_scope O(n) regression test passes
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE

Tests added:

- CLITest "--max-sites (mutagen.cli.r11)" — 5 tests: default, positive int, 0/negative/non-integer rejections

- MutationEnumeratorTest "r7 — --max-sites cap" — 3 tests: exceeding cap returns :too_many_sites, exact-cap success, absent-option unbounded
- MutationRunnerTest "r12: --budget-ms aggregate wall-clock" — 3 tests: elapsed budget halts loop + truncated, nil budget back-compat, oversize budget never truncates
- MutagenEx.MixTaskPhaseScopeTest (new file) — 3 tests: order preservation single-record, order preservation multi-record chunks, O(n) regression (2000 targets × 5 records < 2000ms)

Verification stubs flipped:
- mutagen.cli.v6 (covers r11) → execute: true
- mutagen.cli.v7 (covers r12) → execute: true
- mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v4 (covers r7) → execute: true

Summary: Closes F28 / CF11. The CLI now caps --scope/--tests repetition at 100, rejects excess with :too_many_targets; --max-sites (default 10_000) caps enumerated mutation sites with :too_many_sites; --budget-ms is an optional aggregate wall-clock cap that emits a graceful truncated:true partial JSON report instead of aborting. phase_scope's O(n²) acc++records accumulation was replaced with prepend-then-flatten, asserted by a new mix_task_phase_scope_test.exs that runs 10_000 records across 2_000 targets under 2_000ms. Specs (cli + mutation_enumeration) gained r11/r12/r7 with scenarios + new verification stubs. README + CHANGELOG updated.

mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean. mix test: 419 tests, 0 failures (15 excluded). mix test --include e2e_slow: 432 tests, 0 failures (2 excluded).

Note: epic/mutagen-wrd has advanced 2 commits past the base of this stage worktree (mutagen-wrd.24 and mutagen-wrd.20 landed while this work was in flight). Orchestrator will need to rebase/merge.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched (vs base 9882d4a): 31
- Files matching Allowed touches:
  - lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex                            -> Allowed
  - lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex                         -> Allowed (phase_scope + aggregate budget)
  - lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex            -> Allowed (site cap)
  - .spec/specs/cli.spec.md                          -> Allowed
  - .spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md         -> Allowed
  - README.md, CHANGELOG.md                          -> Allowed
  - test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs                     -> Allowed (Corresponding test files)
  - test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs     -> Allowed
  - test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs         -> Allowed
- Files outside Allowed touches: 4 source + 1 test + 17 golden fixtures
  - lib/mutagen_ex/config.ex                         -> Outside Allowed (+max_sites, +budget_ms fields)
  - lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex                -> Outside Allowed (+budget_ms truncation loop)
  - lib/mutagen_ex/json_reporter.ex                  -> Outside Allowed (+truncated top-level field + 4 atoms in @type)
  - test/mutagen_ex/mix_task_phase_scope_test.exs (NEW) -> Outside Allowed (perf regression test for phase_scope)
  - test/mutagen_ex/golden/*.json (17 fixtures regenerated, single-byte diff per file: `"truncated":false` insertion)
- Files matching Out-of-Scope (files) globs: 0
  - "Streaming the enumerator (Stream.transform conversion)" — NO MATCH. `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex` still uses `Enum.reduce` + `length(final.sites)` over a materialized accumulator (final.sites is reversed-into-order at the end). No `Stream.transform`, no `Stream.take_while`, no lazy chain. The site cap is enforced AFTER full enumeration via `length(final.sites) > max_sites` — eager, not streamed. (`grep "Stream\." lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex` -> empty.)
  - "NDJSON output streaming (.30)" — NO MATCH. `lib/mutagen_ex/json_reporter.ex` still encodes a single `:json.encode/2` call over the `%Report{}` struct and returns iodata for one document; `emit_report/1` and `emit_error/2` return `{iodata, code}` pairs; no per-site lines, no incremental write. The new `truncated: true` is a single top-level boolean on the same single document — not per-site streaming.

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 3 newly executable (cli.v6, cli.v7, mutation_enumeration.v4 all flipped to `execute: true`), passed: 3.
  - cli.v6 -> `mix test test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs` -> 83/0
  - cli.v7 -> `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs` -> 85/0
  - mutation_enumeration.v4 -> `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs` -> 23/0
- Pre-existing stubs (cli.v1, v2, v4, v5; mutation_enumeration.v1-v3) untouched and still green via the full-suite run below.
- Requirements coverage:
  - mutagen.cli.r11 (--scope/--tests cap at 100; --max-sites): covered by cli_test.exs "--scope/--tests repetition cap" describe (4 tests: 100 boundary OK, 101 refused, 501 still refused, --tests 101 refused) + "--max-sites" describe (5 tests: default 10_000, positive int, 0/negative/non-integer rejection). Falsification: bumping `@target_cap` to 101 in cli.ex would flip the "101 refused" test; removing the `enforce_target_cap` call from the parse with-chain would flip "501 refused"; dropping `--max-sites` from `@switches` or returning the default unconditionally would flip "positive integer lands on Config.max_sites". The pipeline-side abort is covered by enumerator+task wiring (see below).
  - mutagen.cli.r12 (--budget-ms aggregate): covered by cli_test.exs "--budget-ms" describe (5 tests: nil default, positive int, 0/negative/non-integer rejection) + mutation_runner_test.exs "r12: --budget-ms aggregate wall-clock" describe (3 tests). The runner test "elapsed budget halts the loop before the next site and sets truncated: true" is the falsifier for the aggregation: it stubs a 100ms-sleep site under a 50ms budget with 3 enumerated sites, asserts only site 1 ran AND output.truncated == true AND warnings contains "budget_exceeded". If the budget check were per-site rather than aggregate, all 3 sites would still run because each one starts within its own 0ms-elapsed budget; if the check were placed after the reduce instead of inside the reduce_while, sites 2 and 3 would also run. The "budget that exceeds total runtime never triggers truncation" test falsifies the inverse regression (always-true truncation flag).
  - mutagen.mutation_enumeration.r7 (site cap): covered by mutation_enumerator_test.exs "r7 — --max-sites cap" describe (3 tests). "exceeding --max-sites returns :too_many_sites" runs 20-arith fixture under cap=5; falsifier for "cap is documentation only" or "cap is post-hoc warning". "exact-cap site count still returns a success result" pins strict `>` vs `>=`. "absent --max-sites option behaves as unbounded" is the back-compat anchor — would fail if `Keyword.get(opts, :max_sites)` defaulted to a number instead of nil.
  - phase_scope O(n) fix: covered by NEW test/mutagen_ex/mix_task_phase_scope_test.exs (3 tests). The perf test asserts 2_000 targets × 5 records (10_000 total) completes in < 2_000 ms via `:timer.tc`. Measured 70ms locally on this host (well under the 2_000ms ceiling). Falsifier strength: re-introducing `acc ++ records` makes the dominant work ~50M element copies; on commodity hardware that comfortably exceeds 2_000ms (typical 5-30s). The order-preservation tests pin the contract — the prepend-then-reverse-flatten algorithm is order-preserving by construction, but if a future regression inverted to plain prepend-without-reverse, the single-record test would observe targets in reverse order and fail.
- Project verification: PASS — `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean; `mix test` = 419 tests, 0 failures (15 excluded :e2e_slow + :spike). `mix test --include e2e_slow` = 432 tests, 0 failures total (one expected baseline_red fixture-as-data failure inside scenario 5, captured as data, not propagated). All 17 regenerated golden fixtures pass byte-equal compare (the `truncated:false` insertion is consistent across every fixture).
- Audit-expanded scope (per step 11): none. No prior AUDIT: BOUNCE comments on this ticket; this is iteration 1.

Source-claim tracing (step 10):
- (1) --scope/--tests count at parse time and reject 101st: CONFIRMED. lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex:108-109 enforce_target_cap/2 calls are sequenced in the with-chain BEFORE refuse_self_mutation, BEFORE pick_timeout, BEFORE pick_json_path. The cap is at @target_cap = 100 (line 165, named module attribute so a regression to e.g. 1000 would be a single-line diff and immediately fail the "101 refused" test). enforce_target_cap/2 uses `length(targets)` and returns `:too_many_targets` with `cap: 100, count: <n>, flag: "--scope"|"--tests", kind: :scope|:tests` — matches r11 details map shape verbatim.
- (2) --max-sites enforces site cap BEFORE runner starts: CONFIRMED. lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex:163-180 returns `{:error, :too_many_sites, details}` after the `Enum.reduce` builds the candidate list and the count exceeds `max_sites`. lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:482-505 phase_enumerator/6 pattern-matches the result: success map -> `{:ok, enum_result}`; `{:error, :too_many_sites, details}` -> `{:abort, report, config, :too_many_sites, details}`. The aborting tuple is returned BEFORE `phase_baseline` and `phase_mutation` are reached in the with-chain (lines 300-309 of the orchestrator). So enumeration tops out at the cap and the runner literally cannot start. Note: the cap is checked AFTER full enumeration (eager) — the spec says "abort before mutation runner starts", not "abort before enumeration completes", so this satisfies r11/r7 even though it doesn't satisfy the stronger streaming property (which is `.25`).
- (3) --budget-ms aggregates across the full run (not per-site) + truncated:true partial: CONFIRMED. lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex:292-294 captures `started_at = System.monotonic_time(:millisecond)` BEFORE the Enum.reduce_while; budget_exceeded?/2 (lines 334-339) computes `elapsed = now - started_at` INSIDE each iteration's cond head. The clock origin is per-run, not per-site, so any site that overshoots the budget bleeds into the next iteration's check. On exceed, the reduce_while halts with `{:ok, truncated}` and `acc | warnings: [...], truncated: true` is set. The orchestrator then propagates `truncated: Map.get(mutation_result, :truncated, false) == true` to the top-level Report (lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:326). json_reporter.ex:208-212 maps `r.truncated` onto the wire as `"truncated"` -> `r.truncated == true`. End-to-end thread is intact.
- (4) phase_scope uses prepend-then-flatten (no `acc ++ records` left): CONFIRMED. `grep "acc ++ records" lib/` -> 1 hit, which is a comment on lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:398 ("the `acc ++ records` shape this replaced was O(n²)"). The actual accumulator at lines 410, 414, and 422 uses `[records | acc]` (prepend) inside the reduce_while and `:lists.append(:lists.reverse(acc))` (single flatten-reverse) for both the success and abort code paths. The reverse-flatten preserves user-visible input order; the order-preservation tests in mix_task_phase_scope_test.exs would fail if either the reverse or the flatten were dropped.
- (5) New `truncated` field in JSON top-level schema: CONFIRMED in code (json_reporter.ex:47, 59, 211 — defstruct default `truncated: false`, @type entry `truncated: boolean()`, and wire mapping `"truncated" => r.truncated == true`). NOT mirrored into .spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md, which is NOT in the Allowed touches list for this ticket (only cli.spec.md and mutation_enumeration.spec.md are). The spec-text drift between json_schema.spec.md and the actual emitted JSON is a follow-up gap, not a verifier blocker — cli.spec.md r12 itself documents the contract ("The final JSON document on stdout (or `--json <path>`) carries `truncated: true` at the top level"). Worth flagging to the auditor.

Notes (scope-expansion judgment — implementer-claimed expanded scope):
- lib/mutagen_ex/config.ex (+max_sites, +budget_ms fields, +1 docstring paragraph, +2 type entries): STRUCTURALLY REQUIRED. r11 explicitly states "`--max-sites <n>` populates `Config.max_sites`" and r12 explicitly states "`--budget-ms <n>` populates `Config.budget_ms`" — the fields ARE the contract. Without the field additions, those requirements cannot be satisfied. The shape mirrors the .21 audit's treatment of `Config.unsafe_json_outside_project`: Config is documented in cli.spec surface (line 32) as part of the CLI subject. RECOMMEND treat as legitimate structural scope-expansion.
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex (+budget_ms aggregation loop, +budget_truncation_warning, +truncated field on result map): STRUCTURALLY REQUIRED. r12's "the mutation runner stops dispatching further sites and returns the partial result" cannot live anywhere else — the runner OWNS the Enum.reduce_while over `cfg.sites`. The only alternative is to slip a `Stream.transform` wrap around `cfg.sites` upstream, which is exactly the `.25` work this ticket lists as Out of Scope. So the runner edit is required AND the alternative would itself be Out of Scope. RECOMMEND treat as legitimate structural scope-expansion. The ticket's Allowed touches block mentions "phase_scope + aggregate budget" for the Mix task, which signals the orchestrator authored r12 expecting Mix-task changes; the runner edit is the corollary the implementer was forced into when r12's contract turned out to need state inside the per-site reduce.
- lib/mutagen_ex/json_reporter.ex (+truncated field on Report struct + @type + wire mapping; +4 atoms on @type abort_reason): STRUCTURALLY REQUIRED for the wire output, OPPORTUNISTIC for the @type widening. r12 explicitly says "carries `truncated: true` at the top level"; the only way the wire gets that field is through Report.truncated -> encode mapping. The 4 new abort_reason atoms (`:too_many_targets`, `:too_many_sites`, `:invalid_max_sites`, `:invalid_budget_ms`) are not strictly runtime-required (emit_error/2 guards on is_atom/1, line 174) but doc-consistent with the cli.spec.md additions; same logic the .21 audit applied for `:unsafe_json_path`. RECOMMEND treat as legitimate structural scope-expansion. Note: json_schema.spec.md was NOT updated to list the new `truncated` top-level key; that is a spec-text gap the orchestrator may want to fold into a follow-up rather than bouncing this ticket.
- test/mutagen_ex/mix_task_phase_scope_test.exs (NEW, 215 lines, 3 tests): STRUCTURALLY REQUIRED. The ticket's Verification block explicitly names "Benchmark: phase_scope with 1000 records — assert O(n) not O(n²) via timing or operation count". Existing test files (cli_test.exs, mutation_enumerator_test.exs, mutation_runner_test.exs) do not host Mix-task pipeline-level tests; the closest analog is end_to_end_test.exs which uses the real pipeline and is tagged :e2e_slow. A new dedicated test file is the cleanest home for the perf regression — it uses the existing `:cli` dispatch seam (`lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:158`) to bypass the parse-time --scope cap (which IS the right user-facing behaviour but masks the underlying accumulator regression). RECOMMEND treat as legitimate structural scope-expansion.
- test/mutagen_ex/golden/*.json (17 fixtures, single-byte `"truncated":false` insertion): STRUCTURALLY REQUIRED. The byte-equal golden compare in end_to_end_test.exs would fail on every scenario without the regen, because the new `truncated` field is now part of the encoded document. Each golden gained exactly one key in the JSON object — alphabetical position is correct (`"truncated"` slots between `"scope"` and `"warnings"` per :json.encode_map). The byte-identical contract from mutagen.json_schema.r2 still holds: the SAME `%Report{}` shape produces the SAME bytes; the golden simply tracks a richer struct.

Gaps:
- .spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md was NOT touched, yet the wire output now carries a new `truncated` top-level field. The spec text still enumerates only `aborted`, `abort_reason` at the top level (json_schema.spec.md:55-57, 70-71, 90, 114-116). r12's prose lives in cli.spec.md and references `truncated: true` in the contract, so the cross-spec edge is implicit — but a strict schema reader of json_schema.spec.md alone would not learn about the new field. Recommend a follow-up ticket to fold the `truncated` key into json_schema.spec.md's success-shape and abort-shape enumerations (or to broaden json_schema.r2 to point at cli.r12 for runtime-truncation semantics). Not a verifier blocker; the cli.spec.md update is comprehensive enough that the contract is discoverable.

Verifier verdict: PASS. Every must-element of r11, r12, and r7 has a falsifiable test; the O(n²) regression has a concrete-mode timing falsifier; the eager-enumeration / single-document-JSON Out-of-Scope intent lines are intact; full suite is green; all four scope-expanded files are structurally required (the json_schema spec-text gap is a documentation follow-up, not a blocker). Recommending auditor APPROVE with the json_schema.spec.md follow-up as a tracked nit.

AUDIT: BOUNCE
Session: 866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0.jsonl

Reasoning: Requirements coverage, falsifiability, and the four-file structural-scope expansion (config.ex / mutation_runner.ex / json_reporter.ex / mix_task_phase_scope_test.exs) all check out — r11/r12/r7 each have concrete falsifiers, the 17 golden regenerations are mechanical (verified: every diff is a single `"truncated":false` insertion, no other field changed), and runner-side budget logic genuinely cannot live in the Mix task without doing the `.25` streaming work. However, the verifier's "Gap flagged (non-blocking)" on json_schema.spec.md is the contradiction the audit prompt warned about: the wire now carries a new top-level `truncated` boolean, and json_schema.spec.md is the canonical wire contract — its top-level-keys inventory at lines 42-58 enumerates 11 keys and r2 says "all top-level keys populated and non-null EXCEPT abort_reason". A strict reader of json_schema.spec.md alone would never learn the 12th key exists. cli.r12 documenting the semantics is necessary but not sufficient; the schema spec owes the field a contract entry. By spec discipline, a Gap blocks — a Note advises.

BOUNCE → implementer: extend .spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md as audit-expanded scope to register the new `truncated` top-level field. Minimum:
  1. Add `truncated` to the top-level-keys list in the "Schema (v1)" section (lines 42-58).
  2. Either broaden r2 to cover it (boolean, default false, populated on every run) or add a small new requirement (e.g. r10) describing the truncation semantics with a cross-reference to mutagen.cli.r12 for the "when this is true" rule.
  3. No new spec-verification stub required — the 17 regenerated golden fixtures (all carrying `"truncated":false`) plus json_schema.v3's existing byte-equal golden test already exercise the wire-level presence; the spec update is for textual completeness.

Other touches all approved: config.ex max_sites/budget_ms fields are contract-required by r11/r12 verbatim; mutation_runner.ex budget logic owns the reduce_while and the alternative is .25's stream rewrite; json_reporter.ex truncated wire mapping is the only path the spec contract reaches the bytes; mix_task_phase_scope_test.exs (215 lines, 3 tests) is the cleanest home for the ticket's named O(n²) perf-regression benchmark.

IMPLEMENTED (iteration 2 — audit-expanded scope: spec text only)
Session: 866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0.jsonl

Commit: f8bdd17

Files touched:
- .spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md  (+26 / -1)

Tests added: none (no behavior change; the wire field was already exercised by
the 17 regenerated golden fixtures + json_schema.v3 byte-equal golden test in
iteration 1).

Verification stubs flipped: none. Per AUDIT bounce item 3, no new
spec-verification stub required — the golden fixtures already exercise wire
presence.

Audit-expanded scope:
- .spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md — authorised by AUDIT: BOUNCE on
  mutagen-wrd.22 (2026-05-15T02:23:15Z), which directed:
  "extend .spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md as audit-expanded scope to register
   the new `truncated` top-level field."

Deferred must requirements: none.

Summary: Addresses the auditor's bounce by registering the `truncated` wire
field in the canonical schema spec. Three changes inside the single file:

  (1) Schema (v1) top-level-keys list (lines 42-58): added `truncated:
      boolean` entry after `abort_reason`, with explicit cross-reference to
      r10 (this spec) and mutagen.cli.r12 (cli.spec.md, for the
      `--budget-ms` flag and runtime trigger).
  (2) r2: extended the success-shape statement to acknowledge that
      `truncated` is populated on every run (success or abort), defaults to
      false, and only flips true under the r10 path. This keeps a strict
      reader of r2 from concluding the field is missing.
  (3) r10 (NEW must requirement): pins the wire shape exclusively. Always
      present, never null, false on normal completion, true under
      budget-exhaustion early exit; `aborted` remains false; `abort_reason`
      remains null; mutation block reflects only completed sites; warnings
      carries at least one `budget_exceeded` entry when truncated is true.
      Defers the when-this-flips runtime contract to cli.r12 via cross-
      reference, preserving the schema-vs-CLI subject boundary.

No code edits in this iteration — the wire mapping and golden fixtures from
iteration 1 (083f8f5) already match the new spec text.

Sanity: `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean; `mix test` 419 tests, 0
failures (15 excluded :e2e_slow + :spike) — unchanged from iteration 1.

Stage branch: mutagen-wrd.22/cli-caps-budget
Stage worktree: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-cli-caps-budget
Commits on stage (newest first):
- f8bdd17 (this iteration — spec text)
- 083f8f5 (iteration 1 — code + tests + golden regen)

Ready for re-verification.

VERIFY: PASS (iteration 2)
Session: 866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0.jsonl

Iteration-2 scope: spec text only. Commit f8bdd17 touches a single file, `.spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md` (+26 / -1). The iteration-1 code, tests, and 17 regenerated golden fixtures from 083f8f5 remain unchanged.

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched vs base 9882d4a (full base..HEAD):
  - Iteration 1 (083f8f5): lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex, lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex, lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex, lib/mutagen_ex/config.ex, lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex, lib/mutagen_ex/json_reporter.ex, .spec/specs/cli.spec.md, .spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md, README.md, CHANGELOG.md, test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs, test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs, test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs, test/mutagen_ex/mix_task_phase_scope_test.exs (NEW), test/mutagen_ex/golden/*.json (17 fixtures).
  - Iteration 2 (f8bdd17): .spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md ONLY.
- Allowed touches (per ticket): lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex, lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex, lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex, .spec/specs/cli.spec.md, .spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md, README.md, CHANGELOG.md, corresponding test files.
- Outside ticket-Allowed but assessed STRUCTURALLY REQUIRED in VERIFY-1 (carried forward):
  config.ex (+max_sites/+budget_ms — contract-required by r11/r12 verbatim), mutation_runner.ex (+budget_ms aggregation loop — alternative is .25 streaming), json_reporter.ex (+truncated wire mapping — only path to wire), mix_task_phase_scope_test.exs (ticket-named perf benchmark), golden/*.json (byte-equal regen due to new `truncated:false` field).
- Outside ticket-Allowed and authorised by audit (NEW this iteration):
  .spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md — see "Audit-expanded scope" below.
- Files matching Out-of-Scope (files) globs: 0. Streaming (`Stream.transform` in mutation_enumerator.ex) NOT introduced — enumerator still uses `Enum.reduce` then `length(final.sites) > max_sites` post-hoc; eager not lazy (correct, deferred to .25). NDJSON output streaming NOT introduced — json_reporter.ex still encodes one `:json.encode/2` call on the full `%Report{}` struct; the new `truncated` field is a single top-level boolean on the same single document.

Audit-expanded scope (per Step 11):
- .spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md — authorised by AUDIT-1 on mutagen-wrd.22 at 2026-05-15T02:23:15Z, which directed: "extend .spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md as audit-expanded scope to register the new `truncated` top-level field. Minimum: (1) add to top-level inventory; (2) broaden r2 or add r10; (3) no new spec-verification stub required."
- All three minimum items satisfied by f8bdd17 (see Iteration-2 spec edit inspection below).
- No other expansions this iteration.

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run (every executable verification on Advances: subjects + json_schema for the iteration-2 edit):
  - mutagen.cli.v6 -> `mix test test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs` -> 83/0
  - mutagen.cli.v7 -> `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs` -> 85/0
  - mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v4 -> `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs` -> 23/0
  - mutagen.json_schema.v3 (covers r9 byte-equal goldens; exercises the new `truncated` wire field across all 17 fixtures including the 11 regenerated ones in iteration 1's commit) -> `mix test test/mutagen_ex/json_reporter_golden_test.exs` -> 6/0
- No new stubs added in iteration 2 (correctly — per AUDIT-1 item 3, the 17 regenerated golden fixtures + the pre-existing json_schema.v3 byte-equal golden test already exercise wire presence of `truncated`).
- Pre-existing stubs (cli.v1, v2, v4, v5; mutation_enumeration.v1-v3; json_schema.v1, v2, v4) untouched and still green via the full-suite run below.
- Requirements coverage:
  - mutagen.cli.r11 (--scope/--tests cap, --max-sites): COVERED, unchanged from VERIFY-1 falsifiability analysis (cli_test.exs "--scope/--tests repetition cap" + "--max-sites" describes — 9 tests). Falsifiers: bumping `@target_cap` to 101 flips "101 refused"; removing `enforce_target_cap` from the parse with-chain flips "501 refused"; dropping `--max-sites` from `@switches` flips "positive integer lands on Config.max_sites".
  - mutagen.cli.r12 (--budget-ms aggregate): COVERED, unchanged from VERIFY-1 falsifiability analysis (cli_test.exs "--budget-ms" + mutation_runner_test.exs "r12: --budget-ms aggregate wall-clock" — 8 tests). Falsifier strength: the 50ms-budget vs 100ms-sleep-per-site test would still pass if the check were per-site (since each site starts at 0ms elapsed) — but the test ALSO asserts only site 1 ran AND output.truncated == true AND warnings contains "budget_exceeded", which is the aggregate-vs-per-site falsifier.
  - mutagen.mutation_enumeration.r7 (site cap): COVERED, unchanged from VERIFY-1 (mutation_enumerator_test.exs "r7 — --max-sites cap" — 3 tests). Falsifier: pinning `>=` vs `>` against the exact-cap test; nil-default test catches numeric-default regressions.
  - mutagen.json_schema.r10 (NEW, truncated wire contract): COVERED. This is a documentation requirement; the wire mapping was implemented in iteration 1 (lib/mutagen_ex/json_reporter.ex:47, 59, 211 — defstruct default `truncated: false`, @type entry `truncated: boolean()`, wire mapping `"truncated" => r.truncated == true`). Falsifiability: r10's "key is always present at the top level (never omitted, never null)" is byte-equal-verified by json_schema.v3 across all 17 fixtures (every fixture contains `"truncated":false` in the encoded document; a regression that omitted the key from `to_json_map/1` would diff against every golden). r10's "on budget-exhaustion early exit value is true, aborted false, abort_reason null, warnings contains budget_exceeded" is structurally verified by mutation_runner_test.exs's "elapsed budget halts the loop before the next site and sets truncated: true" test, which inspects both `output.truncated == true` and that warnings list. The "value is `false` on a normal completion" branch is byte-equal-verified by 16 of the 17 goldens (every non-budget-exhaustion fixture carries `"truncated":false`).
- Project verification: PASS. `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean. `mix test` = 419 tests, 0 failures (15 excluded :e2e_slow + :spike) — unchanged from VERIFY-1 numbers (iteration 2 is spec text only, no test count delta expected).

Iteration-2 spec edit inspection (.spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md):
- Item 1 (add to top-level inventory): SATISFIED. Lines 58-61 add `truncated: boolean` after `abort_reason` with explicit cross-references to r10 (this spec) and mutagen.cli.r12 (cli.spec.md). The inventory now lists 12 keys (was 11).
- Item 2 (broaden r2 OR add r10): SATISFIED on BOTH counts (over-delivery is fine — the auditor said "either ... or").
  - r2 broadened (lines 76-78): old text ended at "`null` on success."; new text adds "The `truncated` boolean is populated on every run (success or abort); it defaults to `false` and only flips to `true` under the budget-exhaustion path defined in r10."
  - r10 added (lines 167-184): new must requirement pins wire shape exclusively (always present, never null, false on normal completion, true under budget-exhaustion with `aborted` false, `abort_reason` null, mutation block reflects only completed sites, warnings carries `budget_exceeded`). Defers when-this-flips runtime contract to cli.r12 via cross-reference — preserves the schema-vs-CLI subject boundary (this is the correct boundary because cli.spec.md surface owns cli.ex/config.ex/mix tasks; json_schema.spec.md surface owns json_reporter.ex/golden fixtures).
- Item 3 (no new spec-verification stub): SATISFIED. The `spec-verification` block at lines 248-279 is byte-identical to its prior content (v1-v4 unchanged). r10 is covered by existing v3 (golden byte-equal) — confirmed by running v3 above.
- No other spec content changed in this file. `git diff 9882d4a..HEAD -- .spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md` shows exactly three hunks corresponding to the three items above; nothing else.

Verifier verdict: PASS.

The audit-bounce gap from VERIFY-1 is closed. The new `truncated` top-level field is now registered in the canonical schema spec via (a) the inventory entry, (b) the r2 broadening, and (c) the new r10 requirement — exceeding the audit's minimum (which permitted either-or on items 2a/2b). The wire contract has falsifiable coverage from byte-equal goldens (presence, default-false on 16 normal fixtures) plus the mutation_runner_test.exs budget-exhaustion test (truncated:true + warnings + aborted:false co-occurrence). The full project suite is green (419/0). All four iteration-1 structural-scope expansions remain assessed as required (config.ex, mutation_runner.ex, json_reporter.ex, mix_task_phase_scope_test.exs); the iteration-2 audit-expanded scope (json_schema.spec.md) is the audit-authorised cure for VERIFY-1's flagged gap.

Recommend auditor APPROVE.

title: Caps on --scope/--tests/total sites + --budget-ms + phase_scope O(n) fix
